metadata.title: 11th Pass Pre-Run Fixes — MLflow 413, Duration Monitoring, Spot Recovery
metadata.priority: P0 — blocks ALL experiment execution until deployed
metadata.estimated-time: 2-4 hours (includes Docker build + push + pulumi up)
metadata.approach: Infrastructure-first: deploy → verify → code changes → rebuild → re-verify
metadata.key-constraint: This plan MUST be executed in ONE session. No "continue in fresh session." Context compaction is acceptable. Session switching is NOT. The fix is NOT DONE until runtime verification passes (Task T8).

context.summary:
The MLflow 413 error has persisted across 10 experiment passes because the fix requires 5 coordinated layers (dependency + Docker + Pulumi + YAML + verification) and each session only completed 1-2 layers before declaring victory.
This plan addresses ALL layers in strict sequence with runtime verification gates. NO task is marked complete until its verification criterion passes.
Reviewer agent identified 2 MISSING prerequisites the original analysis overlooked: (1) Dockerfile.mlflow-gcp must be built and pushed to GAR BEFORE pulumi up (2) enable_cloud_run must be set to true in Pulumi.dev.yaml BEFORE pulumi up
context.reviewer-findings: Critical findings from DevOps reviewer agent: 1. google-cloud-storage is ALREADY a transitive dep via dvc[gs] — explicit addition is defensive (belt-and-suspenders), not the root cause fix 2. The ROOT CAUSE is: enable_cloud_run=false in Pulumi.dev.yaml → Cloud Run MLflow never deployed → no GCS artifact root → HTTP fallback → 413 3. Dockerfile.mlflow-gcp must be built+pushed to GAR BEFORE pulumi up 4. .env must be updated with Cloud Run URL from pulumi stack output 5. Removing file_mounts breaks spot recovery unless checkpoint resume reads from MLflow 6. Old experiments may have stale artifact roots pointing to HTTP

phase.name: Deploy MLflow Cloud Run with GCS Artifacts
phase.description: Deploy the MLflow server to Cloud Run with --no-serve-artifacts and MLFLOW_DEFAULT_ARTIFACT_ROOT=gs://. This is the CORE fix for 413. Requires user to run commands (Docker push, pulumi up need auth).

task.title: Build and push Dockerfile.mlflow-gcp to GAR
task.description: The MLflow server image must exist in GAR before Cloud Run can deploy it. This is the SERVER-SIDE image with --no-serve-artifacts.
task.commands: cd deployment/docker docker build -f Dockerfile.mlflow-gcp -t europe-north1-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess/mlflow-gcp:latest . docker push europe-north1-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess/mlflow-gcp:latest
task.verification: docker manifest inspect europe-north1-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess/mlflow-gcp:latest # Must return a valid manifest with the correct creation date

task.title: Enable Cloud Run in Pulumi config and deploy
task.description: Pulumi.dev.yaml has enable_cloud_run: "false". Must flip to true and run pulumi up. The Pulumi code (commit 893c414) already has the correct config: - MLFLOW_DEFAULT_ARTIFACT_ROOT = gs://minivess-mlops-mlflow-artifacts - --no-serve-artifacts in the Docker entrypoint - Cloud SQL backend store
task.commands: cd deployment/pulumi/gcp pulumi config set minivess-gcp:enable_cloud_run true pulumi up
task.verification: pulumi stack output mlflow_url # Must return a Cloud Run URL like https://minivess-mlflow-xxxxx-lz.a.run.app curl -s $(pulumi stack output mlflow_url)/api/2.0/mlflow/experiments/list # Must return valid JSON (not 404 or error)

task.title: Update .env with Cloud Run MLflow URL
task.description: After pulumi up, the MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI must point to the new Cloud Run URL. SkyPilot reads this from .env via --env-file.
task.commands: # Get the URL from pulumi MLFLOW_URL=$(cd deployment/pulumi/gcp && pulumi stack output mlflow_url) # Update .env (or .env.cloud or whatever the SkyPilot env-file is) # Verify the value grep MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI .env
task.verification: # The URI must be https://, not file:// or http://localhost grep MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI .env | grep -q "https://"

task.title: Verify MLflow Cloud Run records GCS artifact root
task.description: Create a test experiment and verify its artifact_location uses gs://. This confirms the server-side fix is active.
task.commands: uv run python -c " import mlflow import os mlflow.set_tracking_uri(os.environ['MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI']) exp = mlflow.set_experiment('test-gcs-artifact-root') print(f'Artifact location: {exp.artifact_location}') assert exp.artifact_location.startswith('gs://'), f'Expected gs://, got {exp.artifact_location}' print('SUCCESS: MLflow records GCS artifact root') # Clean up mlflow.delete_experiment(exp.experiment_id) "

phase.name: Fix Training Container
phase.description: Ensure the training Docker image has google-cloud-storage explicitly in main dependencies (belt-and-suspenders — it's already transitive via dvc[gs] but explicit is safer). Rebuild and push.

task.title: Add google-cloud-storage to main dependencies explicitly
task.description: Currently in [project.optional-dependencies].sky. Move to [project.dependencies] as a defensive measure. The reviewer confirmed it's already a transitive dep via dvc[gs], but explicit is safer against future dependency changes.

tdd-spec.red: uv run python -c "import google.cloud.storage; print('OK')" # This already passes (transitive dep). The test is that uv lock succeeds.
tdd-spec.green: Add "google-cloud-storage>=3.9.0" to [project.dependencies] in pyproject.toml. Remove from [project.optional-dependencies].sky (avoid duplication). Run: uv lock
tdd-spec.verify: uv run python -c "import google.cloud.storage; print(google.cloud.storage.__version__)" make test-staging # Ensure nothing broke

task.title: Rebuild and push training Docker base image to GAR
task.description: Rebuild Dockerfile.base with the updated dependencies. Push to GAR. Tag with both :latest and :$(git rev-parse --short HEAD) for rollback safety.
task.commands: make build-base-gpu docker tag minivess-base:latest europe-north1-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess/base:latest docker tag minivess-base:latest europe-north1-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess/base:$(git rev-parse --short HEAD) docker push europe-north1-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess/base:latest docker push europe-north1-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess/base:$(git rev-parse --short HEAD)
task.verification: docker manifest inspect europe-north1-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess/base:latest # Verify the image contains google-cloud-storage: docker run --rm minivess-base:latest python -c "import google.cloud.storage; print('GCS OK:', google.cloud.storage.__version__)"
task.note: Image is ~9 GB. Push may take 30-60 min on residential internet.

task.title: Remove competing file_mounts from train_factorial.yaml
task.description:
Remove the GCS checkpoint bucket mount (lines 54-61). This eliminates the competing persistence mechanism (KG invariant violation).
IMPORTANT (reviewer finding): Removing file_mounts means spot recovery can no longer read checkpoints from local disk after preemption. The spot recovery code in train_flow.py:706-719 reads from LOCAL checkpoint_dir. After preemption, that dir is empty (ephemeral disk).
Two options: (a) Keep file_mounts temporarily until spot recovery reads from MLflow (P2 task) (b) Remove file_mounts now and accept that spot preemption = restart from epoch 0
For a 2-epoch debug run, option (b) is acceptable. For production, option (a) is needed. The plan recommends (b) for the 11th pass debug run.

acceptance-criteria.criterion: No reference to gs://minivess-mlops-checkpoints in SkyPilot YAMLs

task.note: For the 2-epoch debug run, spot preemption = restart from epoch 0 is acceptable. The cost of 2 extra epochs (~5 min) is negligible vs the cost of a competing persistence mechanism that masks the real 413 bug.

task.title: RUNTIME VERIFICATION: End-to-end artifact upload test
task.description:
Launch a SINGLE test job (DynUNet, cheapest, fastest) and verify: 1. Logs show GCSArtifactRepository (not HttpArtifactRepository) 2. Checkpoint appears in gs://minivess-mlops-mlflow-artifacts/ 3. No 413 error in logs 4. MLflow UI shows the artifact
Then launch a SINGLE SAM3 Hybrid test job and verify: 5. 900 MB checkpoint uploads without timeout 6. No 413 error
THIS IS THE REAL ACCEPTANCE CRITERION. Everything before this is preparation.
task.commands:
# Launch DynUNet test job cd deployment/skypilot sky jobs launch train_factorial.yaml \ --env MODEL_FAMILY=dynunet \ --env LOSS_NAME=dice_ce \ --env WITH_AUX_CALIB=false \ --env FOLD_ID=0 \ --env MAX_EPOCHS=2 \ --env EXPERIMENT_NAME=test_gcs_upload \ -y
# Wait for completion, then check logs sky jobs logs <job_id> 2>&1 | grep -i "artifact\|GCS\|413\|HttpArtifact"
# Verify artifact in GCS gsutil ls gs://minivess-mlops-mlflow-artifacts/

verification.criterion: Job logs contain NO "413" or "Request Entity Too Large"
gsutil ls shows checkpoint file in GCS bucket
MLflow UI shows the run with artifacts

task.failure-action: If this task FAILS, do NOT proceed to the 11th pass experiment. Debug the failure, fix it, and re-verify. The 413 is the #1 blocker.

task.title: Add expected-duration-minutes to experiment XML schema
task.description:
The factorial-monitor skill needs per-job expected duration to detect anomalies. Add to the experiment XML schema and to the debug factorial config.
Expected durations for debug (2 epochs, half data, 1 fold): - DynUNet: 10 min (5 min training + 5 min setup) - MambaVesselNet: 10 min - SAM3 variants: 25 min (longer weight loading + gradient checkpointing) - Zero-shot: 15 min (no training, just eval) - Max acceptable: 3x expected (30 min DynUNet, 75 min SAM3)

acceptance-criteria.criterion: debug.yaml contains expected_duration_minutes per model family
debug.yaml contains max_duration_minutes per model family

verification.gate: make test-staging (must pass before Docker rebuild)
T8 runtime verification (must pass before 11th pass launch)
Zero "413" or "Request Entity Too Large" in test job logs

anti-patterns-to-avoid.anti-pattern: Marking any task as DONE based on code changes alone
Switching to a fresh session before T8 runtime verification passes
Writing a metalearning doc about 413 without deploying the fix
Running full factorial before single-job verification
Using spot instances for SAM3 in the debug pass (use on-demand)
